skill.name: firebase-remote-config-basics
skill.description: Guide agents through Firebase Remote Config setup, SDK integration, template management, and cautious CLI deployment for Android and iOS apps.
skill.summary: Set up and manage Firebase Remote Config for Android and iOS apps.
skill.author: firebase

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer reported many external command and network patterns, but most are false positives caused by Markdown code spans, SDK examples, and documentation links. The confirmed risk is operational: the skill instructs agents to use Firebase CLI commands, including Remote Config deployment, which can change live app behavior if used without explicit user review. No prompt injection, obfuscation, credential exfiltration, or malicious intent was found in the reviewed files.

medium_findings.title: Firebase CLI Commands Can Modify Live Remote Config
medium_findings.description: The skill gives agents Firebase CLI commands for exporting, deploying, and listing Remote Config templates. These commands are legitimate for this skill, but deployment can change live app behavior and should require explicit user approval and project confirmation.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines contain user-visible Firebase CLI commands, including Remote Config deployment. The risk is operational rather than malicious because the commands match the skill purpose and SKILL.md requires user review before deployment.

low_findings.title: External Command Static Matches Are Mostly Documentation Examples
low_findings.description: Many static findings are Markdown code spans or fenced examples for Gradle, Kotlin, Swift, and Firebase CLI usage. They do not show hidden shell execution, command construction from untrusted input, or background execution by the skill itself.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The evidence is visible Markdown instructional content, not executable code in the skill package. The examples are aligned with normal Firebase setup tasks.

low_findings.title: Documentation URLs Do Not Indicate Data Exfiltration
low_findings.description: The network findings point to Firebase documentation, the Firebase iOS SDK repository, and an XML plist DTD. No evidence was found that the skill sends secrets, project files, or user data to an external endpoint.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are documentation or platform metadata references. I did not find fetch, curl, webhook, analytics beacon, or similar exfiltration logic in the reviewed files.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Static Matches Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The high-severity weak-cryptography alerts correspond to Remote Config wording and SDK feature descriptions, not cryptographic algorithm usage. No MD5, SHA-1, insecure cipher, or custom cryptography implementation was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines describe Firebase Remote Config. They do not contain cryptographic APIs, hashes, ciphers, or security-sensitive transformations.

dangerous_patterns.title: User-Visible External CLI Deployment Pattern
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill includes a Remote Config deployment command that can affect a Firebase project. This pattern is acceptable for a Firebase operations skill when gated by user review and project selection.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The deployment instruction is explicit and visible. It is a real external action risk, but the surrounding text instructs the agent to stop for user review before deployment.

content.value_statement: Remote Config changes can affect production apps without a new release. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code set up SDKs, manage templates, and add review gates before deployment.
actual_capabilities: Guides Android Remote Config dependency setup with Firebase Analytics support.
Guides iOS Remote Config package setup and default plist configuration.
Explains in-app defaults, fetch, activate, and real-time update patterns.
Provides Firebase CLI commands for template export, deployment, and version history.
Includes troubleshooting guidance for npx access and Firebase project context.
limitations: Requires a Firebase project and platform app before full setup can proceed.
Does not replace Firebase Console review or production release governance.
CLI deployment can change live behavior and needs explicit user approval.
SDK version examples may need verification against current Firebase releases.

use_cases.title: Add Feature Flags to a Mobile App
use_cases.description: Set up Remote Config defaults and fetch logic for Android or iOS feature rollout.

use_cases.title: Prepare a Reviewed Config Deployment
use_cases.description: Export a template, edit parameters, request review, deploy, and verify Remote Config history.

use_cases.title: Diagnose Firebase Setup Gaps
use_cases.description: Check missing app config files, project selection issues, and CLI prerequisites before implementation.

prompt_templates.title: Start Remote Config Setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me set up Firebase Remote Config for my app. First identify whether this is Android or iOS, then list the setup steps and files to check.

prompt_templates.title: Add Defaults and Fetch Logic
prompt_templates.prompt: Guide me through adding Remote Config defaults and fetch-and-activate behavior. Explain what should happen before the first server fetch.

prompt_templates.title: Review a Template Change
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me prepare a Remote Config template change for review. Do not deploy until I confirm the project ID and approve the exact change.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Safe Rollout
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a safe Remote Config rollout plan using conditions, defaults, version review, deployment verification, and rollback considerations.

output_examples.input: Set up Remote Config for an Android app.
output: Checks for an existing Firebase app configuration file.
Lists Gradle dependency updates for Remote Config and Analytics.
Explains default values and fetch activation behavior.

output_examples.input: Prepare a feature flag change for deployment.
output: Exports the current template for local review.
Describes the intended parameter and condition changes.
Stops before deployment and asks for explicit approval.

output_examples.output: The skill checks login state, asks for a Firebase project ID when needed, and applies the project flag consistently.

best_practices: Confirm the Firebase project and app before running any CLI command.
Review template changes with the user before deploying Remote Config updates.
Keep in-app defaults aligned with production-safe behavior.
anti_patterns: Deploying Remote Config changes without user approval or project confirmation.
Using remote values without safe local defaults for first launch or offline use.
Treating documentation version examples as always current without verification.

faq.question: Does this skill require the Firebase CLI?
faq.answer: It is best used with the Firebase CLI, but SDK setup guidance can still be followed without running commands.

faq.question: Can it deploy Remote Config changes?
faq.answer: It includes deployment guidance, but it should stop for user review before any live deployment.

faq.question: Does it support Android and iOS?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes separate setup references for Android and iOS Remote Config integration.

faq.question: Does it create Firebase projects automatically?
faq.answer: It can guide project or app creation through Firebase CLI examples, usually with help from the firebase-basics skill.

faq.question: Can it manage feature flags?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains Remote Config parameters, conditions, defaults, fetch behavior, and template deployment flow.

faq.question: What is the main security concern?
faq.answer: The main concern is accidental production impact from CLI deployment, so project confirmation and review are required.
